commit.message:
feat: game room loading state (#31) (#31)

* feat: game room loading state — iris reveal, real data, realtime transition

- Loading state now driven by real data: shown when campaign is active
  but opening_situation is not yet ready (AI still generating)
- Subscribes to game:started broadcast to transition out of loading
- page.tsx fetches session (opening_situation, scene_image_url) and
  passes openingReady + loadingImageUrl props to GameClient
- loadingImageUrl priority: session scene image → world map → world cover
- Redesigned loading state: porthole iris clip-path reveal for image mode,
  rotating astrolabe + pressure gauge arc for no-image fallback
- Teletype dispatch panel with live phase log replaces old progress bar
- Fixed hydration mismatch in MessageBubble (pinned toLocaleTimeString locale)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent game loading race and stabilize message timestamps

* refactor: decouple session readiness query from supabase client

* fix: keep loading state UI out of background image focal area

Collapse the tall dispatch panel into a slim bottom strip — title pushed
to top, single-line phase indicator + progress bar hug the bottom edge,
leaving the iris-revealed image center fully unobstructed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace Date.now() in mock data with static timestamps

Dynamic timestamps caused SSR/client hydration mismatch in MessageBubble
time display. Static ISO strings are stable across renders.

* fix: harden game loading readiness and restore local time display

commit.message:
fix: capture stderr output in NoPtyProcess (closes #17) (#55)

* chore: upgrade starterpack to v1.2.1-dev.9

* #17 merge stdout+stderr via mpsc channel in NoPtyProcess

Refactor NoPtyProcess to spawn two background reader threads (one for
stdout, one for stderr) that forward all data through a shared mpsc
channel. NoPtyProcess::read() now drains the channel, capturing output
from both streams. A leftover buffer handles the case where a received
chunk exceeds the caller-supplied buffer size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* #17 update docs to reflect stderr capture

* #17 add unit tests for stderr capture

Add three NoPtySpawner unit tests that verify stderr is captured and
merged with stdout in the refactored pipe-based process implementation.

* #17: queue implementation plan comment

* chore: clear comment queue [skip ci]

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Light theme + fix AI classification override + forecast diversity

Major fixes:
- Classification: skip Kimi AI batch classification (returned all market/neutral)
  and use keyword-based scorer directly — proven reliable locally and in debug
- Forecast: fix fallback always returning bullish — add symbol-based variance,
  use T+1 returns when all sentiments neutral, diversify T+5 direction
- Key topics now dynamically extracted from news categories and titles

UI overhaul — bright light theme:
- Background: #f8fafd with subtle blue/purple radial gradients
- All surfaces: white/white-alpha with slate borders
- Cards: white with colored borders (emerald/red/blue per sentiment)
- Text: slate-800 primary, slate-500 secondary
- Category buttons: colorful per-category (blue/violet/amber/cyan/orange/emerald)
- Chart: white background, softer grid lines
- Loading overlay: white glass with animated color bars
- Sentiment pills: filled color when active (emerald/red bg with white text)

commit.message:
openclaw: fix dashboard room collisions, sticky presence model, Julia fingerprints

Dashboard: disambiguate rooms sharing names across structures (e.g.,
"Bedroom (Cabin)" vs "Bedroom (XTown)") with automatic collision
detection and hue-shifted colors. Fix chart vertical crush when viewing
both structures by using min-height containers.

Presence: switch from stateless per-scan detection to arrival-based
sticky model — once detected at a location, a person stays until
positively detected at the other location. Fixes Julia flickering to
"unknown" when her phone sleeps between ARP scans.

Update Julia's Crosstown fingerprints (MAC rotated) and add hostname
matching (julias-iphone.lan) which survives iOS MAC/IP rotation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: complete Daktari Dx platform build

Full rebuild of DiagnosticMastery without Replit dependencies using
Next.js 16 + Supabase + Vercel architecture from Ethiopia Clinical
Training platform.

Includes:
- Participant code + email auth with Supabase
- 4-step onboarding (welcome, profile, IRB consent, complete)
- 6 Kenya-contextualized clinical cases (Kibera setting)
- Clinical vignette display with bilingual chief complaints
- Cognitive checkpoint (bias-reduction step)
- Differential diagnosis with 5-min timer, ranking, confidence slider
- 4-component scoring (inclusion, ranking, not-to-miss, calibration)
- IRT 3PL adaptive case selection engine
- Swahili-named achievements and 10-level gamification
- Dashboard with domain cards, stats, achievement preview
- Analytics, achievements, and profile pages
- Bottom navigation (mobile-first)
- Admin case viewer and CSV/JSON data export
- Kenya Green + Medical Blue theme
- Supabase migrations with RLS policies
- PWA manifest for offline capability
- CFK Africa + CollectiveGood co-branding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Handle EncryptedSharedPreferences corruption crash (AEADBadTagException)

Bazı kullanıcılarda cihaz yedekten geri yüklendiğinde veya Android
güncellemesi sonrası, EncryptedSharedPreferences'ın şifre anahtarı
geçersiz hale geliyor. Bu durumda SDK AEADBadTagException fırlatıp
uygulamayı crash ettiriyordu (Fatal - uygulama hiç açılmıyordu).

Fix: SessionManager artık 3 katmanlı korumaya sahip:
1. Normal şekilde EncryptedSharedPreferences oluştur
2. Hata varsa corrupt dosyayı sil, yeniden oluştur
3. Hala hata varsa fallback olarak normal SharedPreferences kullan

Etkilenen kullanıcı yeni bir user_id alır ama uygulama crash olmadan açılır.

Crashlytics issue: 7a955e77241272a7fc21325f2434a0b2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use HEROKU_BUILD_COMMIT with HEROKU_SLUG_COMMIT as fallback (#1220)

Heroku deprecated HEROKU_SLUG_COMMIT in favor of HEROKU_BUILD_COMMIT.
Update the default release detection to check HEROKU_BUILD_COMMIT first,
keeping HEROKU_SLUG_COMMIT as a fallback for backwards compatibility with
older Heroku stacks.

Fixes GH-1218 and GO-125

fix: correct EngineConfig criteria type to list[CriterionInput] (#31)

* docs: add structured criteria documentation and example YAML configs

Add comprehensive documentation for semantic and command criteria, their mixed usage patterns, and timeout configurations. Include three new example YAML files demonstrating CI pipeline, code quality, and database migration task patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct EngineConfig criteria type from list[str] to list[CriterionInput] (Closes #30)

This fixes a type inconsistency where EngineConfig.criteria was declared as list[str] but the engine actually expects list[CriterionInput] (which can be strings or structured command criteria dicts). The fix includes:

1. Update EngineConfig.criteria field type to list[CriterionInput]
2. Export CriterionInput from config module
3. Add --command-timeout CLI option support
4. Update CLI main.py to handle both string and structured criteria types
5. Add comprehensive unit tests for the --command-timeout option
6. Remove obsolete Issue #30 notes from example YAML configs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move criteria_to_str_list to models and DRY test helpers

- Move criteria_to_str_list from engine.py to models/criteria.py
  for better module cohesion, re-export from models/__init__.py
- Simplify EngineConfig construction in CLI (direct constructor)
- Extract duplicated _create_completed_result methods into
  module-level make_completed_result function in test_cli.py

fix(add_prometheus_target): use temp file to avoid read/write race condition

Reading and writing the same file simultaneously with sed | tee truncates
the content. Now copies to a temp file first, applies sed on the copy,
and writes the result back.

commit.message:
fix: eliminate duplicate AI calls from intra-session job dedup

RWFA and Remote.com list the same job under multiple URLs. The DB
dedup only filters jobs already stored, so within a single scrape
session cross-page duplicates each triggered an AI request.

Add seen_hashes set (title hash) in both scrapers to skip duplicates
before AI classification. Also change scrape cron from hourly to daily
at midnight.

Configure production deployment for biu.lingkunganplus.com

ARCHITECTURE:
- Existing biulms-ssl nginx container handles SSL for biu.lingkunganplus.com
- Our app runs as container named 'biulms' on port 8007 (bridge network)
- biulms-ssl proxies all traffic to biulms:8007 (no separate nginx needed)
- SSL certificates already provisioned via Let's Encrypt

CHANGES:
- docker-compose.prod.yml:
  - Container name: 'biulms' (matches existing SSL nginx proxy target)
  - Port: 8007 (matches existing nginx proxy config)
  - Network: bridge (same as biulms-ssl)
  - ALLOWED_ORIGIN: https://biu.lingkunganplus.com
  - Health check: uses node HTTP client (curl/wget not in slim image)
  - Removed separate nginx service (handled by existing biulms-ssl)
- Dockerfile: expose port 8007 (SERVER_PORT default)
- .env.local on server: ALLOWED_ORIGIN updated to https://biu.lingkunganplus.com

commit.message:
feat: Wake Lock API — keep screen on during processing

Acquires navigator.wakeLock.request('screen') whenever any tool is
processing. This prevents the device from sleeping and the browser
from suspending network activity mid-upload/compression.

- Watches all processing states (compress, GIF, trim, watermark,
  speed, remove audio, extract audio, bulk, cartoon)
- Re-acquires lock on visibilitychange if user briefly switches apps
  and comes back while still processing
- Releases automatically when processing completes
- Silently no-ops on browsers that don't support Wake Lock API

commit.message:
Fix Camel JSON parse error caused by unescaped quotes in dish names

Claude sometimes wraps dish names in ASCII double-quotes (e.g. "Camel"),
which breaks JSON parsing. Instruct Claude to never use quotes in names.
